name: ideal-string-a2
description: Ideal (stiffness-free) string fixed at both ends, A2. Partials are the exact harmonic series 110 Hz * n, and the mode shapes are analytic: phi_n(x) = sin(n * pi * x) for x in [0, 1] along the string. The maps below are that formula sampled on a 9-point uniform grid, so they are checkable by hand rather than fitted to anything. This example exists to exercise position: striking at x = 0.5 cannot excite the even partials at all (sin(2 pi * 0.5) = 0), which is why a string plucked dead centre sounds hollow, and picking up at a node of a partial mutes that partial no matter how it was struck.
